introduction: This spec defines all non-admin CLI commands for the `akc` tool — login (OAuth flow orchestration), user profile management, API key lifecycle, PAT management, and organization browsing — each implemented as a thin Cobra command delegating to the Go SDK (spec 12) and built on the CLI Core infrastructure (spec 13). Note: requirements referencing Go SDK client methods (e.g., `client.GetProviders`, `client.ExchangeOAuthCode`) are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against the installed spec-12 SDK before implementation.

glossary.1:1 command: A CLI command that maps directly to a single SDK method and API endpoint. All commands in this spec except `login` are 1:1 commands.
glossary.APIKeyFull: Go SDK type representing a full API key including its plaintext key string, returned by `RefreshKey`.
glossary.APIKeyMeta: Go SDK type representing API key metadata (no plaintext key), returned by `ListKeys`.
glossary.AuthCallbackRequest: Go SDK type used to exchange an OAuth authorization code for credentials, containing provider, code, redirect_uri, and expires fields.
glossary.CreateTokenRequest: Go SDK type carrying name, permissions, and expires fields for creating a Personal Access Token.
glossary.ENDPOINT_URL: Environment variable that supplies the server endpoint URL, recognized by CLI Core alongside the `--endpoint-url` flag and config file.
glossary.NewAuthenticatedClient: CLI Core function that constructs an authenticated SDK client. Reads `endpoint_url` and `api_key` from config/env/flags and pre-validates that `api_key` is present before returning. Returns exit code 2 if `api_key` is absent.
glossary.NewKeysCmd: Constructor function returning the Cobra parent command for `akc keys`.
glossary.NewLoginCmd: Constructor function returning the Cobra command for `akc login`.
glossary.NewOrgsCmd: Constructor function returning the Cobra parent command for `akc orgs`.
glossary.NewTokensCmd: Constructor function returning the Cobra parent command for `akc tokens`.
glossary.NewUserCmd: Constructor function returning the Cobra parent command for `akc user`.
glossary.Organization: Go SDK type representing an organization object.
glossary.PAT: Go SDK type representing a Personal Access Token's metadata (no plaintext token value).
glossary.PATFull: Go SDK type representing a Personal Access Token including its plaintext token value — available only at creation time.
glossary.Response: Go SDK generic wrapper type `Response[T]` whose `.Data` field holds the typed payload returned by certain SDK read methods.
glossary.RevokeKeyResponse: Go SDK type representing the response from revoking an API key.
glossary.UpdateUserRequest: Go SDK type carrying the `full_name` field for updating a user's profile.
glossary.api_key: The full API key string stored in the config file, formatted as `<prefix>_<key_id>_<secret>`. Used for authenticating SDK calls.
glossary.atomic write: Write to a temporary file then `os.Rename` into place, preventing partial writes from corrupting the config file. Implemented by CLI Core (spec 13).
glossary.authorize_url: The OAuth authorization URL returned by the server's provider discovery endpoint. May already contain query parameters such as `client_id` that must be preserved.
glossary.callback server: A temporary local HTTP server started by the `login` command to receive the OAuth redirect from the browser. Binds to `127.0.0.1` on a random port and returns HTTP 404 for all paths except `/callback`.
glossary.codeCh: A `chan string` used by the OAuth callback server handler to send the authorization code to the main login goroutine on successful callback.
glossary.composite command: A CLI command that involves multiple API calls; distinguished from 1:1 commands in `help --json` by the `composite: true` flag. In this spec, only `login` is composite.
glossary.config-mutating command: A CLI command that writes to the config file (path defined by CLI Core, spec 13). In this spec: `login`, `keys refresh`, `keys revoke`. All use atomic writes via CLI Core's save function.
glossary.endpoint_url: The base URL of the apikit server, stored in the config file and used to construct the SDK client.
glossary.errCh: A `chan error` used by the OAuth callback server handler to send an error to the main login goroutine on state mismatch or callback failure.
glossary.helpers.go: File in `internal/cli/` containing shared helper functions: `validateExpires`, `parsePermissions`, `parseKeyID`, `openBrowser`.
glossary.key_id: The identifier segment of an API key. Extracted from the full key string by parsing `<prefix>_<key_id>_<secret>` — the penultimate segment when split on `_`. Parsing implemented in `helpers.go`.
glossary.loginOpts: Struct or parameter set passed to `runLogin` encapsulating the login command's configuration (provider, expires, endpoint URL, etc.).
glossary.loginTimeoutSeconds: Package-level constant in `login.go` set to 120, representing the maximum seconds the login command waits for the browser OAuth callback before timing out.
glossary.openBrowser: Shared helper function in `helpers.go` that opens a URL in the default browser using platform-appropriate commands.
glossary.parseKeyID: Shared helper function in `helpers.go` that extracts `key_id` from a full API key string.
glossary.parsePermissions: Shared helper function in `helpers.go` that splits a comma-separated permissions string, trims whitespace, and returns a non-empty slice or an error.
glossary.redirect_uri: The local callback URL (`http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback`) passed to the OAuth authorization URL and to `ExchangeOAuthCode` so the server can verify it matches the original request.
glossary.runLogin: Internal helper function implementing the login OAuth flow. Accepts a `timeout time.Duration` parameter so tests can reduce the timeout without modifying the `loginTimeoutSeconds` constant.
glossary.state parameter: A cryptographically random string (64 hex chars) included in the OAuth authorization request and validated on callback to prevent CSRF attacks.
glossary.user_id: The authenticated user's identifier stored in the config file after a successful login.
glossary.validateExpires: Shared helper function in `helpers.go` that validates an `--expires` integer is one of 0, 30, 60, or 90.
glossary.xdg-open: Linux command used to open a URL in the system's default browser.
glossary.*cobra.Command: The Cobra library type representing a CLI command. Each command constructor returns a `*cobra.Command` configured with Use, Short, Long, Args, RunE, and flags.
glossary.Use: Cobra `*cobra.Command` field specifying the command's usage string (e.g., `"show"`).
glossary.Short: Cobra `*cobra.Command` field providing a short one-line description of the command.
glossary.Long: Cobra `*cobra.Command` field providing a detailed description of the command.
glossary.Args: Cobra `*cobra.Command` field specifying positional argument validation (e.g., `cobra.ExactArgs(1)`).
glossary.RunE: Cobra `*cobra.Command` field holding the function that executes when the command is invoked. Returns `error`.
glossary.ExactArgs(1): Cobra argument validator requiring exactly one positional argument.
glossary.MarkFlagRequired: Cobra method that marks a flag as required, causing Cobra to return an error if the flag is not provided.
glossary.init(): Go `init()` function — not used for command registration in this spec; commands are registered explicitly via `AddCommand` in `main.go`.
glossary.rootCmd.AddCommand: Cobra method that registers child commands on the root command.
glossary.cli.NewLoginCmd(): Call to `NewLoginCmd()` from the `internal/cli` package, returning the login Cobra command.
glossary.cli.NewUserCmd(): Call to `NewUserCmd()` from the `internal/cli` package, returning the user parent Cobra command.
glossary.cli.NewKeysCmd(): Call to `NewKeysCmd()` from the `internal/cli` package, returning the keys parent Cobra command.
glossary.cli.NewTokensCmd(): Call to `NewTokensCmd()` from the `internal/cli` package, returning the tokens parent Cobra command.
glossary.cli.NewOrgsCmd(): Call to `NewOrgsCmd()` from the `internal/cli` package, returning the orgs parent Cobra command.
glossary.composite: true: Help-json annotation value indicating a composite command (multiple API calls).
glossary.method: null: Help-json annotation value indicating no single HTTP method (composite command).
glossary.path: null: Help-json annotation value indicating no single API path (composite command).
glossary.auth: \"none\": Help-json annotation value indicating no authentication required.
glossary.auth: \"api_key\": Help-json annotation value indicating API key authentication is required.
glossary.--provider: Login command flag specifying the OAuth provider name (default: `"github"`).
glossary.--expires: Flag specifying credential expiry in days; valid values are `0`, `30`, `60`, `90` (default: `90`).
glossary.--full-name: User update command flag specifying the user's display name (required).
glossary.--name: Tokens create command flag specifying the PAT's human-readable label (required).
glossary.--permissions: Tokens create command flag specifying comma-separated `resource_type:action` pairs (required).
glossary.\"github\": Default value for the `--provider` flag on the login command.
glossary.{}: Empty JSON object printed to stdout by commands that have no response body (e.g., `tokens revoke`).
glossary.apikit.NewClient(endpointURL): SDK constructor call creating an unauthenticated client for the login flow.
glossary.client.GetProviders(ctx): SDK method fetching available OAuth providers from the server.
glossary.client.ExchangeOAuthCode(ctx, &apikit.Au: SDK method exchanging an OAuth authorization code for user credentials.
glossary.client.GetUser(ctx): SDK method fetching the authenticated user's profile.
glossary.client.UpdateUser(ctx, &apikit.UpdateUse: SDK method updating the authenticated user's full name.
glossary.client.ListKeys(ctx): SDK method listing the authenticated user's API keys.
glossary.client.RefreshKey(ctx, keyID): SDK method refreshing the authenticated user's API key (new secret, same key_id).
glossary.client.RevokeKey(ctx, keyID): SDK method revoking the authenticated user's API key.
glossary.client.ListTokens(ctx): SDK method listing the authenticated user's PATs.
glossary.client.CreateToken(ctx, &apikit.CreateTo: SDK method creating a new PAT with the specified name, permissions, and expiry.
glossary.client.GetToken(ctx, tokenID): SDK method fetching metadata for a specific PAT.
glossary.client.RevokeToken(ctx, tokenID): SDK method revoking a specific PAT (returns only error, HTTP 204).
glossary.client.ListUserOrgs(ctx): SDK method listing organizations the authenticated user belongs to.
glossary.client.GetOrg(ctx, id): SDK method fetching a specific organization's details.
glossary.client.ListOrgMembers(ctx, id): SDK method listing members of a specific organization.
glossary.RefreshKey: SDK method on `*Client` that refreshes an API key, returning `*APIKeyFull`.
glossary.response.Data: The typed payload field on `Response[T]`, accessed after SDK methods that return `*Response[T]`.
glossary.response.User: The `User` field on `AuthCallbackResponse`, containing the authenticated user's profile after login.
glossary.response.User.ID: The UUID of the authenticated user, extracted from the login response for config storage.
glossary.response.APIKey.Key: The full API key string from the login response, stored in the config file.
glossary.response.Key: The full API key string from the `APIKeyFull` response, used to update the config file after key refresh.
glossary.[]*APIKeyMeta: Go slice type returned by `ListKeys` — array of API key metadata objects.
glossary.[]*Organization: Go slice type returned by `ListUserOrgs` — array of organization objects.
glossary.[]*PAT: Go slice type returned by `ListTokens` — array of PAT metadata objects.
glossary.[]*User: Go slice type returned by `ListOrgMembers` — array of user objects.
glossary.[]string: Go slice type for parsed permissions list.
glossary.User: Go SDK type representing a user profile (canonical shared type from `sdk_types.go`).
glossary.error: Go's built-in error interface, returned by SDK methods and helper functions.
glossary.crypto/rand: Go standard library package for cryptographically secure random number generation.
glossary.crypto/rand.Read(32 bytes): Call to read 32 cryptographically random bytes for OAuth state parameter generation.
glossary.hex.EncodeToString: Go standard library function encoding bytes as a lowercase hexadecimal string.
glossary.state: The OAuth CSRF protection parameter — a 64-char hex string generated by the CLI and validated on callback.
glossary.client_id: OAuth client identifier — already present in the `authorize_url` from the server; the CLI does not append it separately.
glossary.response_type=code: OAuth query parameter requesting an authorization code grant.
glossary./callback: The HTTP path on the local callback server that handles the OAuth redirect.
glossary./favicon.ico: Example of a stray browser request path that the callback server returns HTTP 404 for.
glossary.GET /callback: The HTTP request method and path handled by the local callback server for OAuth redirects.
glossary.127.0.0.1:0: TCP address binding the callback server to localhost on a random available port.
glossary.http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback: The OAuth redirect URI constructed from the callback server's dynamically assigned port.
glossary.codeCh chan string: Go channel used by the callback handler to send the authorization code to the main goroutine.
glossary.errCh chan error: Go channel used by the callback handler to send errors (e.g., state mismatch) to the main goroutine.
glossary.ctx.Done(): Go context cancellation signal — fires when the login timeout expires or the context is cancelled.
glossary.context.WithTimeout(context.Background(): Fresh Go context with 5-second deadline used for graceful callback server shutdown.
glossary.time.Duration: Go standard library type representing a duration of time.
glossary.http.Server.Shutdown: Go standard library method for gracefully shutting down an HTTP server.
glossary.parseKeyID(apiKey): Call to the `parseKeyID` helper function to extract `key_id` from a full API key string.
glossary.parsePermissions(permissionsFlag): Call to the `parsePermissions` helper function to split and validate the permissions flag value.
glossary.parts[len(parts)-2]: Go expression extracting the penultimate element from a slice — used to get `key_id` from a key split on `_`.
glossary._: Underscore character used as the delimiter in API key format `<prefix>_<key_id>_<secret>`.
glossary.exec.Command(\"open\", url): Go `os/exec` call to open a URL in the default browser on macOS.
glossary.exec.Command(\"xdg-open\", url): Go `os/exec` call to open a URL in the default browser on Linux.
glossary.runtime.GOOS == \"darwin\": Go runtime check for macOS platform.
glossary.runtime.GOOS == \"linux\": Go runtime check for Linux platform.
glossary.json.MarshalIndent: Go standard library function for producing indented JSON output.
glossary.os.Rename: Go standard library function used in atomic writes — renames a temp file into place.
glossary.runLogin(ctx context.Context, timeout ti: Signature of the internal login helper function accepting a configurable timeout for testability.
glossary.runLogin(ctx, time.Duration(loginTimeout: Call to `runLogin` from the Cobra `RunE` function, passing the constant-derived timeout.
glossary.loginTimeoutSeconds = 120: Package-level constant defining the default login timeout as 120 seconds.
glossary.time.Duration(loginTimeoutSeconds) * tim: Expression converting the integer timeout constant to a `time.Duration` for `runLogin`.
glossary.id: Positional argument representing a UUID (org ID) in `orgs show` and `orgs members` commands.
glossary.token_id: Positional argument representing a PAT identifier in `tokens show` and `tokens revoke` commands.
glossary.resource_type:action: Permission format used by PATs — a colon-separated pair (e.g., `users:read`, `orgs:read`).
glossary.,: Comma delimiter used to separate permissions in the `--permissions` flag value.
glossary.akc login: CLI command that runs the OAuth login flow (composite command).
glossary.akc tokens revoke: CLI command that revokes a specific PAT by `token_id`.
glossary.<html><body><h1>Login successful</h1><p>: Verbatim HTML response returned by the callback server on successful OAuth callback.
glossary.<html><body><h1>Login failed</h1><p>OAut: Verbatim HTML response returned by the callback server on OAuth state mismatch (HTTP 400).
glossary.\"Opening browser for authentication...\: Stderr message printed before attempting to open the browser for OAuth login.
glossary.\"Open this URL in your browser: <url>\": Stderr fallback message printed when the browser cannot be opened automatically.
glossary.\"Logged in as <username>\": Stderr success message printed after a successful OAuth login.
glossary.\"API key refreshed\": Stderr message printed after a successful API key refresh.
glossary.\"API key revoked. Run 'akc login' to ob: Stderr message printed after revoking the API key.
glossary.\"Token created. Save the token value —: Stderr warning printed after creating a PAT, reminding the user to save the plaintext token.
glossary.\"Token <token_id> revoked\": Stderr message printed after revoking a PAT.
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"--e: Error envelope JSON for invalid `--expires` flag value (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"end: Error envelope JSON for missing endpoint URL during login (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"--p: Error envelope JSON for empty `--permissions` flag value (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"inv: Error envelope JSON for an API key string that cannot be parsed (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"log: Error envelope JSON for login timeout (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"OAu: Error envelope JSON for OAuth state validation failure (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"fai: Error envelope JSON template for config write failure (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"no: Error envelope JSON for missing API key on authenticated commands (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":404,\"message\":\"p: Error envelope JSON for an unrecognized OAuth provider (exit code 1).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"OAu: Error envelope JSON for OAuth state validation failure (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"end: Error envelope JSON for missing endpoint URL during login (exit code 2).
glossary.{\"error\":{\"code\":2,\"message\":\"no: Error envelope JSON for missing API key on authenticated commands (exit code 2).

user_story.goal: register all command groups with the root command via explicit AddCommand calls in main.go
user_story.benefit: avoid init() side effects and import cycles while keeping the command tree visible

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `rootCmd.AddCommand` with the results of `cli.NewLoginCmd()`, `cli.NewUserCmd()`, `cli.NewKeysCmd()`, `cli.NewTokensCmd()`, and `cli.NewOrgsCmd()` — no `init()` functions are used for registration
populates `Use`, `Short`, `Long`, `Args`, `RunE`, flag definitions, and help-json annotations (name, description, method, path, args, flags, auth, composite) on the returned `*cobra.Command`
sets `composite: true`, `method: null`, and `path: null` in its help-json annotation, and `auth: "none"`
set `auth: "api_key"` in their help-json annotation and include a non-null HTTP method and API path

user_story.goal: run `akc login` to authenticate via browser-based OAuth and persist credentials
user_story.benefit: obtain an API key stored in the config file so all subsequent commands work without re-authenticating

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts `--provider` flag (string, default `"github"`) and `--expires` flag (int, default `90`, valid values `0`, `30`, `60`, `90`)
constructs an unauthenticated SDK client via `apikit.NewClient(endpointURL)` and calls `client.GetProviders(ctx)` to fetch available OAuth providers
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: proceeds to provider lookup; exits with code 1 and error JSON if the SDK call fails
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked with a valid `endpoint_url` and valid flags

acceptance_criteria.action: searches the list for the requested provider by name; if not found exits with code 1 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":404,"message":"provider '<name>' not found"}}`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 1 with error envelope to stdout if provider absent
acceptance_criteria.trigger: provider list returned by the server

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a 64-character hex `state parameter` using `crypto/rand.Read(32 bytes)` encoded with `hex.EncodeToString`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: 64-character lowercase hex string

acceptance_criteria.action: starts a `callback server` bound to `127.0.0.1:0`, derives `redirect_uri` as `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback`, and handles only `GET /callback`; all other paths receive HTTP 404 without triggering shutdown

constructs the authorization URL by parsing the provider's `authorize_url`, retrieving its existing query parameters, and adding `redirect_uri`, `state`, and `response_type=code` without overwriting existing parameters and without adding `client_id`

prints `"Opening browser for authentication..."` to stderr, then opens the authorization URL via `openBrowser`; on `openBrowser` failure prints `"Open this URL in your browser: <url>"` to stderr and continues

creates `codeCh chan string` and `errCh chan error`, wraps the flow in `runLogin(ctx, time.Duration(loginTimeoutSeconds)*time.Second, opts)`, and the main goroutine selects on `codeCh`, `errCh`, and `ctx.Done()`

responds with HTTP 200 and the verbatim success HTML `

Login successful

You may close this tab.

`, then sends the code on `codeCh`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with verbatim success HTML; code delivered on `codeCh`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: GET /callback request arrives with matching state and a code

acceptance_criteria.action:
sends an error on `errCh`, responds with HTTP 400 and the verbatim error HTML `

Login failed

OAuth state mismatch. Please try again.

`; main goroutine cancels context and exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"OAuth state mismatch — possible CSRF attack"}}`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400 with verbatim state-mismatch HTML to browser; exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: GET /callback request arrives with a mismatched state

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `client.ExchangeOAuthCode(ctx, &apikit.AuthCallbackRequest{Provider: provider, Code: code, RedirectURI: redirectURI, Expires: &expires})`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: proceeds to config save on success; exits code 1 with error envelope on SDK error

acceptance_criteria.action: saves `endpoint_url`, `user_id` (from `response.User.ID`), and `api_key` (from `response.APIKey.Key`) to config via CLI Core's atomic write function; prints `response.User` as JSON to stdout; prints `"Logged in as <username>"` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: user JSON to stdout; success message to stderr; exit code 0

acceptance_criteria.action: shuts down the `callback server` via `http.Server.Shutdown` using a fresh `context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: graceful completion or error after callback

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"login timed out waiting for browser callback"}}`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with timeout error envelope to stdout
acceptance_criteria.state: `ctx.Done()` fires before a callback is received

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"endpoint URL is required for login — use --endpoint-url or set ENDPOINT_URL"}}`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no API calls made
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `endpoint_url` is absent from flags, env, and config

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no API calls made

edge_cases.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"failed to save config: <detail>"}}` to stdout; credentials are NOT printed; user must re-run `akc login`
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 2 with config-failure error envelope; no credential output
edge_cases.error_condition: config write fails after successful code exchange

edge_cases.action: responds with HTTP 404; does not signal `codeCh` or `errCh`; server continues listening
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 404 to browser; neither channel receives a value
edge_cases.trigger: a request arrives at any path other than `/callback` (e.g., `/favicon.ico`)

edge_cases.action: `runLogin` returns a timeout error; command exits with code 2 and the timeout error envelope; the `callback server` is shut down
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 2 with timeout error envelope; server shut down
edge_cases.state: `loginTimeoutSeconds` has elapsed without any valid `/callback` request

edge_cases.action: prints `"Open this URL in your browser: <url>"` to stderr and continues the login flow; browser failure is not fatal
edge_cases.return_contract: fallback URL printed to stderr; login flow continues

edge_cases.action: shutdown uses a fresh `context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)` to ensure graceful teardown regardless of the login context state
edge_cases.error_condition: context is cancelled (e.g., after state mismatch) before `http.Server.Shutdown` is called

user_story.goal: run `akc user show` to retrieve my profile
user_story.benefit: inspect my current user data without opening a browser or dashboard

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts no flags or positional arguments; constructs an authenticated SDK client via `NewAuthenticatedClient`; calls `client.GetUser(ctx)`; prints `response.Data` as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `User` JSON to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: `NewAuthenticatedClient` returns an error; command exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}` to stdout without making any SDK call
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no network request

user_story.goal: run `akc user update --full-name "..."` to change my display name
user_story.benefit: update profile information from the command line without client-side format constraints

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts `--full-name` flag (string, Cobra `MarkFlagRequired`); performs no client-side validation of the value; calls `client.UpdateUser(ctx, &apikit.UpdateUserRequest{FullName: fullName})`; prints the returned `User` as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with updated `User` JSON to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: surfaces the server's error envelope as JSON to stdout and exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 1 with server error envelope JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: server returns a 4xx response (e.g., 422 for empty or invalid name)

user_story.goal: run `akc keys list` to view all my API keys
user_story.benefit: audit active keys without accessing the web UI

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts no flags or arguments; calls `client.ListKeys(ctx)`; prints the returned `[]*APIKeyMeta` array as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `[]*APIKeyMeta` JSON array to stdout

user_story.goal: run `akc keys refresh` to rotate my current API key
user_story.benefit: get a new API key and have it automatically saved to the config without manual editing

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts no flags or arguments; uses `NewAuthenticatedClient` to verify `api_key` presence; calls `parseKeyID(apiKey)` to extract `key_id`; calls `client.RefreshKey(ctx, keyID)`; on success updates `api_key` in config with `response.Key` via CLI Core atomic write; prints `APIKeyFull` as indented JSON to stdout; prints `"API key refreshed"` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `APIKeyFull` JSON to stdout; config updated atomically

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"invalid API key format"}}` before calling the SDK
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no network request
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `api_key` string has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments

edge_cases.action: exits with code 2 and a descriptive error envelope; the new key value is NOT printed
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 2 with config-failure error envelope to stdout
edge_cases.error_condition: config write fails after successful `RefreshKey` response

user_story.goal: run `akc keys revoke` to invalidate my current API key
user_story.benefit: immediately revoke access and have the config cleared so the key cannot be accidentally reused

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts no flags or arguments; uses `NewAuthenticatedClient` to verify `api_key` presence; calls `parseKeyID(apiKey)` to extract `key_id`; calls `client.RevokeKey(ctx, keyID)`; on success clears `api_key` and `user_id` to empty strings in config via CLI Core atomic write; prints `RevokeKeyResponse` as indented JSON to stdout; prints `"API key revoked. Run 'akc login' to obtain a new key."` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `RevokeKeyResponse` JSON to stdout; `api_key` and `user_id` cleared in config

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"invalid API key format"}}` before calling the SDK
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no network request
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `api_key` string has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments

user_story.goal: run `akc tokens list` to view all my Personal Access Tokens
user_story.benefit: inspect active PATs from the CLI without accessing the web dashboard

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts no flags or arguments; calls `client.ListTokens(ctx)`; prints the returned `[]*PAT` array as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `[]*PAT` JSON array to stdout

user_story.goal: run `akc tokens create --name "..." --permissions "..." [--expires N]` to create a new PAT
user_story.benefit: obtain a scoped token for automation, with the plaintext token displayed exactly once

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts `--name` (string, Cobra `MarkFlagRequired`), `--permissions` (string, Cobra `MarkFlagRequired`, comma-separated), and `--expires` (int, default `90`, valid values `0`, `30`, `60`, `90`); calls `parsePermissions(permissionsFlag)` to produce `[]string`; validates `--expires` via `validateExpires`; calls `client.CreateToken(ctx, &apikit.CreateTokenRequest{Name: name, Permissions: perms, Expires: &expires})`; prints `PATFull` as indented JSON to stdout; prints `"Token created. Save the token value — it cannot be retrieved later."` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `PATFull` JSON to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"--permissions must not be empty"}}` without calling the SDK
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no network request
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `--permissions` resolves to an empty slice after splitting and trimming

acceptance_criteria.action: exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}` without calling the SDK
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no network request

user_story.goal: run `akc tokens show <token_id>` to retrieve metadata for a specific PAT
user_story.benefit: inspect a token's name, permissions, and expiry without logging into the web dashboard

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts exactly one positional argument `token_id` (Cobra `ExactArgs(1)`); calls `client.GetToken(ctx, tokenID)`; prints `response.Data` as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `PAT` JSON to stdout

user_story.goal: run `akc tokens revoke <token_id>` to invalidate a specific PAT
user_story.benefit: immediately revoke a compromised or unnecessary token from the CLI

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts exactly one positional argument `token_id` (Cobra `ExactArgs(1)`); calls `client.RevokeToken(ctx, tokenID)` which returns only `error`; on success prints `{}` as JSON to stdout; prints `"Token <token_id> revoked"` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `{}` to stdout; revocation message to stderr

user_story.goal: run `akc orgs list` to view all organizations I belong to
user_story.benefit: discover available organizations without accessing the web UI

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts no flags or arguments; calls `client.ListUserOrgs(ctx)`; prints the returned `[]*Organization` array as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `[]*Organization` JSON array to stdout

user_story.goal: run `akc orgs show <id>` to retrieve details for a specific organization
user_story.benefit: inspect organization metadata by UUID from the CLI

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts exactly one positional argument `id` (org UUID, Cobra `ExactArgs(1)`); calls `client.GetOrg(ctx, id)`; prints `response.Data` as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `Organization` JSON to stdout

user_story.goal: run `akc orgs members <id>` to list all members of an organization
user_story.benefit: audit organization membership from the CLI

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts exactly one positional argument `id` (org UUID, Cobra `ExactArgs(1)`); calls `client.ListOrgMembers(ctx, id)`; prints the returned `[]*User` array as indented JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0 with `[]*User` JSON array to stdout

user_story.goal: use `parseKeyID` from `helpers.go` to extract `key_id` from any valid `api_key` string
user_story.benefit: avoid duplicating parsing logic across `keys refresh` and `keys revoke` and enable independent unit testing

acceptance_criteria.action: splits the `api_key` string on `_`, returns `parts[len(parts)-2]` as `key_id` (penultimate segment), handling prefixes with or without embedded underscores
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(string, error)` — non-empty `key_id` string or error if fewer than 3 segments

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an error indicating invalid key format
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `("", error)` with descriptive error message
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `api_key` string has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments

user_story.goal: use `validateExpires` from `helpers.go` for consistent `--expires` validation across login and tokens create
user_story.benefit: guarantee the same valid values (0, 30, 60, 90) are enforced in all commands without duplication

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts an int and returns nil if the value is one of 0, 30, 60, 90; returns a descriptive error otherwise
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `error` — nil for valid values; non-nil for all others

user_story.goal: use `parsePermissions` from `helpers.go` to split and validate the `--permissions` flag value
user_story.benefit: ensure consistent parsing and empty-check logic across commands and enable independent unit tests

acceptance_criteria.action: splits the input string on `,`, trims whitespace from each entry, discards empty entries, and returns the resulting `[]string`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `([]string, error)` — non-empty slice on success; error if resulting slice is empty

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an error; individual entry format (e.g., `resource_type:action`) is not validated
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(nil, error)` with message `"--permissions must not be empty"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: input is an empty string or contains only whitespace and commas

user_story.goal: use `openBrowser` from `helpers.go` to open a URL in the user's default browser
user_story.benefit: isolate platform-specific browser launch logic for testability and avoid duplication

acceptance_criteria.action: on `runtime.GOOS == "darwin"` runs `exec.Command("open", url)`; on `runtime.GOOS == "linux"` runs `exec.Command("xdg-open", url)`; returns an error if the command fails
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `error` — nil on success; non-nil if the platform command fails or GOOS is unsupported

requirements.title: Authentication Pre-validation for All Authenticated Commands

user_story.goal: receive an immediate exit code 2 when `api_key` is missing, before any network request is made
user_story.benefit: get fast, clear feedback that login is required rather than a confusing server 401 error

acceptance_criteria.system: all authenticated commands (user, keys, tokens, orgs)
acceptance_criteria.action: delegate client construction to `NewAuthenticatedClient`; if `api_key` is absent, the command exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}` without making any SDK call
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2 with error envelope to stdout; no network request

user_story.goal: receive all successful command output as indented JSON to stdout
user_story.benefit: parse and process results programmatically without additional formatting steps

acceptance_criteria.action: format successful output using `json.MarshalIndent` with two-space indentation and no HTML escaping, writing to stdout; write all human-readable messages to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: valid indented JSON on stdout; plain-text messages on stderr

acceptance_criteria.action: on API error (4xx/5xx), write the server's error envelope JSON to stdout and exit with code 1; on client error, write the CLI-generated error envelope JSON to stdout and exit with code 2
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: error envelope JSON to stdout; appropriate exit code

user_story.goal: ensure config-mutating commands (`login`, `keys refresh`, `keys revoke`) use the CLI Core atomic write function
user_story.benefit: prevent partial writes that would corrupt the config file and leave the user in an unrecoverable state

acceptance_criteria.action: call CLI Core's atomic write function (write to temp file, `os.Rename` into place); only the fields listed for each command are changed; all other config fields are preserved

edge_cases.action: command exits with code 2 and a descriptive error envelope; any data that was to be printed to stdout (e.g., new key) is NOT printed
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 2 with config-failure error envelope to stdout
edge_cases.error_condition: CLI Core atomic write function returns an error

user_story.goal: call `runLogin` directly with a short timeout to exercise timeout behavior in tests
user_story.benefit: avoid waiting 120 seconds in tests while keeping the production `loginTimeoutSeconds` constant unmodified

acceptance_criteria.action: defines `loginTimeoutSeconds = 120` as a package-level constant and implements the OAuth flow in `runLogin(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration, opts loginOpts) error`; the Cobra `RunE` function calls `runLogin` passing `time.Duration(loginTimeoutSeconds) * time.Second`; the constant is never modified

correctness_properties.title: State Parameter Is Cryptographically Random and Correctly Sized
correctness_properties.invariant: the `state parameter` is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters derived from 32 bytes of `crypto/rand` output

correctness_properties.title: Existing authorize_url Query Parameters Are Preserved
correctness_properties.for_any: OAuth authorization URL constructed by the login command
correctness_properties.invariant: every query parameter present in the provider's `authorize_url` (including `client_id`) appears unchanged in the final authorization URL; `redirect_uri`, `state`, and `response_type=code` are added without overwriting any existing parameter

correctness_properties.title: Callback Server Does Not Shut Down on Non-/callback Paths
correctness_properties.for_any: HTTP request to the `callback server` at any path other than `/callback`
correctness_properties.invariant: the server responds with HTTP 404; neither `codeCh` nor `errCh` receives a value; the server continues listening

correctness_properties.title: Credentials Not Emitted on Config Write Failure
correctness_properties.for_any: login flow where code exchange succeeds but config write fails
correctness_properties.invariant: stdout contains only the error envelope; no user JSON or credential data is written to stdout

correctness_properties.title: parseKeyID Always Returns Penultimate Segment
correctness_properties.for_any: `api_key` string with 3 or more underscore-delimited segments
correctness_properties.invariant: `parseKeyID` returns `parts[len(parts)-2]` regardless of the number of underscores in the prefix

correctness_properties.title: No API Call Made When api_key Is Absent
correctness_properties.for_any: invocation of any authenticated command without a configured `api_key`
correctness_properties.invariant: no network request is issued; the command exits with code 2 before any SDK method is called

correctness_properties.title: validateExpires Accepts Exactly {0, 30, 60, 90}
correctness_properties.invariant: the function returns nil if and only if the value is one of 0, 30, 60, 90; all other values return a non-nil error

correctness_properties.title: Shutdown Context Is Always Fresh
correctness_properties.for_any: any login flow termination (success, timeout, or state mismatch)
correctness_properties.invariant: the `callback server` shutdown call always uses a fresh `context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)` and never reuses the login context

correctness_properties.title: tokens revoke Always Emits Valid JSON to stdout
correctness_properties.invariant: stdout contains exactly `{}` (a valid JSON object) even though the SDK returns no response body

correctness_properties.title: loginTimeoutSeconds Constant Is Never Modified
correctness_properties.for_any: any invocation of the login command or `runLogin`
correctness_properties.invariant: `loginTimeoutSeconds` remains 120 at all times; tests reduce the timeout by passing a short `time.Duration` argument directly to `runLogin`, not by modifying the constant

steps.action: runs `akc login --provider github --expires 90 --endpoint-url https://api.example.com`
validates `endpoint_url` is present and `--expires` is valid via `validateExpires`
constructs unauthenticated SDK client via `apikit.NewClient(endpointURL)` and calls `client.GetProviders(ctx)` to retrieve available OAuth providers
sends GET /providers to the apikit server and returns the provider list
finds the `github` provider in the list; generates 64-char hex `state parameter` via `crypto/rand`
starts `callback server` on `127.0.0.1:0`; derives `redirect_uri`; constructs authorization URL by appending `redirect_uri`, `state`, `response_type=code` to provider's `authorize_url`
prints `"Opening browser for authentication..."` to stderr; calls `openBrowser(authURL)`
executes `open <authURL>` (macOS) or `xdg-open <authURL>` (Linux) via `os/exec`
navigates to the authorization URL, authenticates with the OAuth provider, and is redirected to `redirect_uri` with `code` and `state` query parameters
receives GET /callback; validates `state` matches; responds with HTTP 200 success HTML; sends `code` on `codeCh`
receives from `codeCh`; calls `client.ExchangeOAuthCode(ctx, &apikit.AuthCallbackRequest{Provider: "github", Code: code, RedirectURI: redirectURI, Expires: &expires})`
sends POST to the OAuth callback endpoint and returns user and `api_key` credentials
calls CLI Core atomic write function (spec 13) to save `endpoint_url`, `user_id`, and `api_key` to the config file
writes config to a temp file and renames it atomically into place
prints `response.User` as indented JSON to stdout; prints `"Logged in as <username>"` to stderr; shuts down `callback server` with a 5-second background context
observes user JSON on stdout and success message on stderr; subsequent `akc` commands use the saved `api_key`

execution_paths.title: Authenticated Command — User Show Happy Path

steps.action: runs `akc user show`
calls CLI Core `NewAuthenticatedClient` to construct an authenticated SDK client, pre-validating `api_key` is present
reads `endpoint_url` and `api_key` from config/env/flags and returns an authenticated client
calls `client.GetUser(ctx)`
sends authenticated GET /user to the apikit server and returns `*Response[User]`
prints `response.Data` as indented JSON to stdout; exits with code 0
observes user profile JSON on stdout

execution_paths.title: Keys Refresh — Rotate API Key and Save to Config

steps.action: runs `akc keys refresh`
calls `NewAuthenticatedClient` to verify `api_key` presence; calls `parseKeyID(apiKey)` to extract `key_id`
splits `api_key` on `_`, returns penultimate segment as `key_id`
calls `client.RefreshKey(ctx, keyID)`
sends authenticated POST/PUT to the key refresh endpoint and returns `*APIKeyFull`
calls CLI Core atomic write to update `api_key` in config with `response.Key`
atomically writes updated config file
prints `APIKeyFull` as indented JSON to stdout; prints `"API key refreshed"` to stderr; exits with code 0
observes new key JSON on stdout; config now contains the refreshed `api_key`
runs `akc login` but never completes the browser OAuth flow
starts `callback server` and opens browser; enters select on `codeCh`, `errCh`, `ctx.Done()`
`loginTimeoutSeconds` (120s in production, short in tests) elapses; `ctx.Done()` fires
exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"login timed out waiting for browser callback"}}` to stdout; shuts down `callback server`
observes timeout error envelope on stdout; no credentials are saved

execution_paths.title: Tokens Create — PAT Created with Permissions

steps.action: runs `akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions "users:read,orgs:read" --expires 30`
calls `NewAuthenticatedClient`; calls `parsePermissions("users:read,orgs:read")` returning `["users:read","orgs:read"]`; calls `validateExpires(30)` returning nil
splits on `,`, trims whitespace, returns non-empty `[]string`
calls `client.CreateToken(ctx, &apikit.CreateTokenRequest{Name: "ci-bot", Permissions: perms, Expires: &expires})`
sends authenticated POST to the tokens endpoint and returns `*PATFull`
prints `PATFull` as indented JSON to stdout; prints `"Token created. Save the token value — it cannot be retrieved later."` to stderr; exits with code 0
observes full token JSON (including plaintext token) on stdout; saves the token value

execution_paths.title: Missing API Key — Pre-validation Fires Before Network

steps.action: runs `akc orgs list` without a configured `api_key`
calls `NewAuthenticatedClient`
finds `api_key` absent in config, env, and flags; returns an error immediately without making any network request
exits with code 2 and error JSON `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}` to stdout
observes error envelope on stdout; no HTTP request was made to the server

error_handling.condition: `endpoint_url` is absent from flags, env, and config when `akc login` is invoked
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"endpoint URL is required for login — use --endpoint-url or set ENDPOINT_URL"}}` to stdout; no API calls are made

error_handling.condition: `--expires` flag value is not one of 0, 30, 60, 90
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}` to stdout; no API calls are made

error_handling.condition: requested OAuth provider name is not in the list returned by `client.GetProviders`
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 1 and writes `{"error":{"code":404,"message":"provider '<name>' not found"}}` to stdout

error_handling.condition: OAuth callback received with a `state parameter` that does not match the generated value
error_handling.behavior: `errCh` receives the mismatch error; browser receives HTTP 400 with verbatim state-mismatch HTML; main goroutine cancels context and exits with code 2 and `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"OAuth state mismatch — possible CSRF attack"}}` to stdout

error_handling.condition: login context times out (`loginTimeoutSeconds` elapsed) before any valid `/callback` request
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"login timed out waiting for browser callback"}}` to stdout; `callback server` is shut down

error_handling.condition: CLI Core atomic write fails after successful OAuth code exchange
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"failed to save config: <detail>"}}` to stdout; no credential data is written to stdout

error_handling.condition: `api_key` is absent from config, env, and flags when any authenticated command is invoked
error_handling.behavior: `NewAuthenticatedClient` returns an error; command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}` to stdout without making any SDK call

error_handling.condition: `api_key` string has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments when `keys refresh` or `keys revoke` is invoked
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"invalid API key format"}}` to stdout without making any SDK call

error_handling.condition: `--permissions` flag resolves to an empty slice after splitting and trimming
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"--permissions must not be empty"}}` to stdout without making any SDK call

error_handling.condition: server returns a 4xx or 5xx response for any command
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 1 and writes the server's error envelope JSON to stdout

error_handling.condition: network error occurs during any SDK call
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes `{"error":{"code":2,"message":"<network error description>"}}` to stdout

error_handling.condition: `openBrowser` subprocess fails (e.g., `xdg-open` not installed or no display server)
error_handling.behavior: login command prints `"Open this URL in your browser: <url>"` to stderr and continues the login flow; browser open failure is not fatal

error_handling.condition: server returns 4xx for `akc user update` (e.g., 422 for empty or invalid name)
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 1 and writes server's error envelope JSON to stdout; no client-side validation was performed

error_handling.condition: CLI Core atomic write fails after successful `keys refresh` response
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes a descriptive error envelope to stdout; the new key value is NOT printed

error_handling.condition: CLI Core atomic write fails during any `config-mutating command`
error_handling.behavior: command exits with code 2 and writes a config-failure error envelope to stdout; data that would have been printed on success is NOT printed

symbols.name: Command
symbols.signature: type Command struct { Use string; Short string; Long string; Args PositionalArgs; RunE func(cmd *Command, args []string) error; ... }
symbols.notes: Central Cobra type; each command constructor returns *cobra.Command

symbols.name: ExactArgs
symbols.notes: Used for commands with required positional arguments (tokens show, tokens revoke, orgs show, orgs members)

symbols.name: MarkFlagRequired
symbols.notes: Used to mark --full-name, --name, --permissions as required flags

symbols.name: Read
symbols.notes: Used to generate 32 random bytes for the OAuth state parameter

symbols.name: EncodeToString
symbols.notes: Encodes 32 random bytes to a 64-character lowercase hex state parameter

symbols.name: Server
symbols.signature: type Server struct { Addr string; Handler Handler; ... }
symbols.notes: Used for the local OAuth callback server

symbols.name: Server.Shutdown
symbols.notes: Gracefully shuts down the callback server; called with a fresh 5-second background context

symbols.name: ListenAndServe
symbols.notes: Starts the callback server; or net.Listen + srv.Serve for :0 port binding

symbols.name: Command
symbols.notes: Used in openBrowser to invoke `open` (macOS) or `xdg-open` (Linux)

symbols.name: MarshalIndent
symbols.signature: func MarshalIndent(v any, prefix, indent string) ([]byte, error)
symbols.notes: Used for all JSON output; called with prefix="" and indent="  " (two spaces)

symbols.name: NewEncoder
symbols.notes: Alternative encoder; SetEscapeHTML(false) must be called to disable HTML escaping
